reasoning: As of recent medical sources through mid-to-late 2025 (such as StatPearls publications in May 2025), there are no vaccines available for any of the Borrelia species that cause relapsing fever. There are no ongoing clinical trials for a human relapsing fever vaccine, either. Since vaccine development, clinical testing, and regulatory approval invariably take several years, it is virtually impossible for a new vaccine to be developed, approved, and listed on the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page within the 1-to-3 month period spanning the resolution dates (November 2025 to February 2026). As probabilities must fall between 0.05 and 0.95, I am using the lowest possible baseline, slightly increasing for later dates in accordance with standard forecasting uncertainty principles.
